pr.title: fix path issues on Windows in tools/report_score.py
pr.author: BR-

summary: Fixed a Windows-specific path/subprocess issue in tools/report_score.py by using the standard Python sys module, running the subprocess through sys.executable, and adding explicit subprocess failure reporting before parsing JSON output.
what_changed: Replaced `from elftools.common.py3compat import sys` with the standard-library `import sys` in tools/report_score.py.
Changed the subprocess result handling from directly reading `.stdout.strip()` into storing the completed process as `proc`.
Passed `executable=sys.executable` to `subprocess.run`, grounding the child process invocation in the currently running Python interpreter.
Added a nonzero return-code check that prints the command, stdout, and stderr to stderr, then exits with status 1.
Only parses JSON from `proc.stdout.strip()` after confirming the subprocess succeeded.
smart_moves: Using `sys.executable` avoids relying on a path spelling or environment-specific Python launcher behavior, which is especially useful for Windows compatibility.
Switching to the real standard-library `sys` module makes `sys.executable`, `sys.stderr`, and `sys.exit` unambiguous rather than depending on pyelftools compatibility imports.
The fix improves debuggability: failed subprocess calls now expose the attempted command plus captured stdout and stderr instead of falling through to JSON parsing errors.
decomp_lessons: Project maintenance scripts should avoid hardcoded or POSIX-centric Python executable assumptions; use `sys.executable` when a helper needs to run under the same interpreter or virtual environment.
When tooling expects machine-readable JSON from a subprocess, check the subprocess return code before parsing to avoid hiding the real failure behind JSON decode errors.
Small cross-platform fixes in support tooling matter for contributor workflow because score/report scripts are part of the decompilation feedback loop.
naming_conventions: Renamed the local subprocess variable from direct `result` assignment to `proc` while the raw stdout is still a process object, then reused `result` for the stripped stdout/parsed JSON value.
follow_up_queries: Find other tooling scripts that import `sys` from `elftools.common.py3compat` instead of the standard library.
Search for subprocess calls in tools/ that parse stdout as JSON without checking `returncode`.
Audit Python tooling for hardcoded POSIX paths or virtualenv path assumptions that could fail on Windows.
